data.name: Neuralink N1
data.invasiveness: Minimally invasive - 1,024 electrodes on flexible threads inserted by surgical robot
data.status: First human implant January 2024; second implant July 2024; clinical trials ongoing
data.clinical_trial: FDA IDE approved for PRIME study (quadriplegic patients)
data.description: Neuralink's N1 implant features 1,024 electrodes on 64 threads thinner than human hair, inserted by a specialized surgical robot. First human patient Noland Arbaugh demonstrated chess playing and cursor control. The device is coin-sized, wireless, and rechargeable. Thread retraction issues in the first patient reduced effective channels but were partially mitigated by software.

data.name: BrainGate2
data.invasiveness: Invasive - silicon microelectrode arrays penetrating cortex 1-1.5mm
data.developer: BrainGate consortium (Brown University, MGH, Stanford, Case Western)
data.status: Clinical trials ongoing since 2004; longest-running BCI human trial
data.description: The longest-running human BCI trial. BrainGate2 has demonstrated that paralyzed patients can control computer cursors, robotic arms, and tablets using thought alone. Recent breakthroughs include high-rate handwriting decoding (90 characters/min) and speech decoding. Utah arrays have durability limitations (gliosis after ~5 years).

data.name: Stentrode
data.invasiveness: Minimally invasive - inserted via jugular vein, deployed in superior sagittal sinus
data.status: First human implant 2019 (Australia); FDA IDE approved 2021; US clinical trial ongoing
data.description: Synchron's Stentrode is the only endovascular BCI - it's deployed through blood vessels without open brain surgery. The nickel-titanium stent-electrode array is positioned in the superior sagittal sinus adjacent to motor cortex. Patients have achieved text generation, internet browsing, and Apple Vision Pro control. Less signal resolution than cortical implants but dramatically safer.

data.name: ECoG Speech Decoder (UCSF)
data.invasiveness: Invasive - subdural electrode grid placed on brain surface (no penetration)
data.status: Clinical research; demonstrated 62 words/min speech decoding (2023)
data.description: Edward Chang's lab at UCSF achieved a landmark in 2023: decoding attempted speech from a paralyzed patient at 62 words per minute using ECoG arrays. The system decodes phonemes from motor cortex activity and synthesizes speech with a voice matching the patient's pre-injury voice. This approaches natural conversation speed (~150 words/min).

data.name: NeuroPace RNS
data.invasiveness: Invasive - implanted in skull with cortical strip and depth leads
data.status: FDA approved 2013 for refractory epilepsy; 1,000+ implants
data.clinical_trial: FDA PMA approved; post-market studies ongoing
data.description: The only FDA-approved closed-loop BCI. The RNS System continuously monitors brain activity and delivers targeted electrical stimulation when it detects seizure patterns. Clinical data shows 50%+ seizure reduction in responsive patients. The device records EEG data that helps physicians optimize treatment.

data.name: Paradromics Connex Direct
data.status: First human implant planned 2025; FDA Breakthrough Device designation
data.clinical_trial: FDA IDE approved for ALS patients
data.description: Paradromics is developing the highest-channel-count BCI with 65,536 electrodes. The Connex Direct uses microwire arrays for high-bandwidth neural recording. FDA granted Breakthrough Device designation. The company aims to enable naturalistic communication for ALS patients at conversational speeds.

data.name: Precision Neuroscience Layer 7
data.invasiveness: Minimally invasive - flexible micro-electrode film slipped between skull and brain surface
data.status: Temporary human testing demonstrated; working toward FDA approval
data.clinical_trial: Pre-clinical; temporary intraoperative testing in humans
data.description: Precision's Layer 7 cortical interface is a flexible film that conforms to the brain surface without penetrating tissue. It's designed to be reversible - the implant can be removed without damaging brain tissue. The company demonstrated recording during tumor resection surgeries. Ben Rapoport co-founded Neuralink before starting Precision.

data.name: Meta EEG Typing BCI
data.description: Meta demonstrated that non-invasive EEG can decode imagined handwriting at unprecedented speeds for non-invasive systems. Using deep learning, they achieved ~12 words/min from EEG signals. While far slower than invasive systems, this shows non-invasive BCI is improving and could benefit patients who cannot undergo surgery.
